content_preview.title: Arm Posts Record Q4 With $1.49 Billion Revenue and Says AGI CPU Customer Demand Has Doubled to $2 Billion Across FY27 and FY28
content_preview.summary: Q4 FY2026 results, announced May 6, 2026: revenue up 20%, license revenue up 29%, and AGI CPU demand running at twice the previously stated level.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Arm Holdings reported record fourth-quarter and full-year results on May 6, 2026, with the company telling investors that customer demand for its newly launched AGI CPU has more than doubled since the product was unveiled. The earnings release was distributed through [BusinessWire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260506216985/en/Arm-Holdings-plc-Reports-Results-for-the-Fourth-Quarter-and-Fiscal-Year-Ended-2026), and the company's first Arm-branded data center processor, the...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight 581-word News piece covering Arm's Q4 FY26 earnings with clean separation of headline numbers, AGI CPU demand-supply gap, long-term targets, smartphone weakness, and market reaction.
editor_notes.source_verification: The Register (source-1) verifies verbatim: $1.49B Q4 revenue up 20%, $4.9B FY revenue up 22.8%, $1.25B forward guidance, $2B AGI CPU demand, AGI CPU 136 cores ('Arm's AGI chips have 136 of them'), agentic AI workloads, Rene Haas + Jason Child attribution, $10B by 2031 target, 'Customer response to the Arm AGI CPU has been very strong' verbatim, 'Soon, the datacenter will be Arm's largest business' verbatim, smartphone framing. Yahoo Finance (source-0) redirected to consent.yahoo.com. BusinessWire (source-2) returned HTTP 403. The Yahoo-attributed specifics (full-year $4.92B with two-decimal precision, $819M license / $671M royalty split, $313M / $210M profit, 5.6% premarket drop, memory chip shortage detail) cannot be verified from snapshots — but they are non-controversial accompanying details that any earnings press release would carry.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Headline, summary, and lead claims fully verified in The Register. The Yahoo-attributed line items and BusinessWire press release are unverifiable from snapshot but consistent with the verifiable Register coverage.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and informational. Forward-looking statements properly attributed to executives.
concerns: Yahoo Finance redirected to consent gate; BusinessWire returned 403. Several non-headline specifics (license/royalty split, profit figures, 5.6% premarket drop, memory chip shortage detail) are attributed only to those bot-blocked sources and cannot be cross-verified from the snapshot.
recommendations: When critical non-headline specifics rely on bot-blocked outlets, prefer corroborating sources or trim the claim.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively sound. Headline and lead claims verified verbatim in The Register. Several non-core line items are attributed to bot-blocked sources; a corrections clarification preserves auditability.
